pages.name: My Files
pages.description: Homepage and main file browser showing the user’s files and folders with sidebar navigation, search, sharing, and file actions. This page also hosts special folders like Documents, Projects, Videos, and Reports and is the hub for most organizational tasks.
primary_functions: View root files and folders and navigate into subfolders such as Documents, Projects, Videos, and Reports
Use left sidebar to jump to key sections (My Files root, Documents, Projects, Videos, Reports, Recent, Shared With Me, Storage, Trash)
Create new folders (e.g., Invoices 2024, Q3 Reports) and upload files
Search within the current folder using the local search bar above the file list and filter results by file type, date, and other basic filters
Use the global search bar in the header to start a full‑account search that opens the Search Results page
Select files and folders to move, copy, rename, star/unstar, delete, restore, or make available offline using the toolbar and context menus
Open the sharing panel from context menus to invite people, configure roles (Viewer, Editor), set link access, and define expiration dates
Open files or folders by clicking their names, including navigating to special folders like Reports > Q3 Reports or Projects > Client Pitch

incoming_params.description: ID of the folder whose contents should be displayed. If omitted, the My Files root folder is shown.
Well-known folder key such as 'documents', 'projects', 'videos', or 'reports'. When present, the page resolves this via getSpecialFolder and loads that folder.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a folder row in the file list or a folder segment in the breadcrumb to navigate into that folder.
Click 'Documents', 'Projects', 'Videos', or 'Reports' in the left sidebar to jump to a special folder.
Click a file name or thumbnail in the My Files list to open it in the File Viewer.
Submit the global search form in the header while on My Files.

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'My Files' header navigation link and by clicking the site logo.
Default landing page at index.html when opening the site.
Folder-specific or special-folder views accessed via folderId or specialFolderKey query parameters.

pages.name: Search Results
pages.description: Search results page for global or scoped searches with advanced filtering, sorting, and bulk actions. Also reused as the 'View all files' storage cleanup view.
primary_functions: Display results for global searches (e.g., 'Q3', 'Roadmap.docx') or folder‑scoped searches initiated from My Files
Filter results by file type (e.g., PDF, spreadsheet, video), last modified date, size, and other available criteria
Sort results by name, size, last modified, or last opened to surface the newest or largest items first
Select multiple files and folders from the results and perform actions such as move, copy, delete, share, star, or make available offline
Open selected files in the File Viewer or navigate to their parent folders in My Files
Support “View all files” scenarios (e.g., from Storage) by showing the complete account file list with filters and sorting options

incoming_params.description: Free-text search query (e.g., 'Q3', 'Roadmap.docx'). An empty or wildcard value can be used for 'View all files' storage scenarios.
Origin of the search such as 'global_search', 'storage', or 'scoped_search'; used to adjust UI hints and back links.
When set to 'storage_cleanup', indicates the page is being used as the Storage 'View all files' view optimized for size-based cleanup.
Sort direction for results, typically 'asc' or 'desc'.
Encoded filter state (file type, date range, size thresholds) so the search can be reloaded/bookmarked with the same filters.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click on a file result to open it in the File Viewer.
Choose 'Open containing folder' from a result item’s context menu to switch to that folder in My Files.
Adjust filters or sort options on the Search Results page, causing the URL to update and the results to be reloaded.

access_methods.description: Accessed when running a global search or a storage 'View all files' action, which navigate to search.html with appropriate query and mode parameters.

pages.name: Storage & Usage
pages.description: Overview of storage usage with tools to find and manage large files across the account. Serves as the starting point for size-based cleanup workflows.
primary_functions: Show overall storage usage, including usage by folder (Documents, Projects, Videos, etc.) and by file type (documents, videos, images, etc.)
Provide a summarized list of the largest files with a quick way to inspect their size, type, and location
Offer a “View all files” or similar control that opens a full storage file list (reusing the Search Results layout) filtered to all content
Allow filtering the storage file list by file type (e.g., show only video files) and sorting by size (largest first) to identify large items over a size threshold
Enable selection of large files to move them into archive folders (such as Videos > Archive) or delete them
Provide navigation from storage entries back to their parent folders in the My Files view

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click the 'View all files' button in the Storage & Usage overview to open a full sortable list of files optimized for cleanup.
Click a top-level folder tile (e.g., 'Videos') in the 'usage by folder' section to open that folder in My Files.
Click on one of the largest files in the summary list to inspect it in the File Viewer.

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Storage' header navigation link.
Can be opened directly via storage.html for quick access to storage insights.

pages.name: Recent Items
pages.description: List of recently opened items with filters and offline access controls, helping users quickly resume work or mark important files for offline availability.
primary_functions: Display files and folders ordered by last opened date so the most recent activity appears at the top
Filter recent items by file type (e.g., only PDFs) and optionally by other simple attributes
Sort by last opened, name, or last modified to adjust the order of recent items
Select one or more items and toggle “Available offline” on or off from the toolbar
Open recent items directly or navigate to their containing folders in My Files for further organization

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a recently opened file to open it in the File Viewer.
Use 'Show in folder' or similar action on a recent item to jump to its containing folder in My Files.

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Recent' header navigation link.

pages.name: Shared With Me
pages.description: View and manage items that have been shared with the user, including permissions, details, and labels such as 'Confidential'.
primary_functions: Display all files and folders that others have shared with the user in a tabular or card view
Sort shared items by shared date (newest first), name, owner, or last opened to quickly locate the newest shared file
Open the context menu for a shared file to access actions such as open, share, view details, or add to My Files if supported
Open the sharing panel for a selected item to adjust permissions, including disabling download for viewers when allowed
Open a details or information sidebar for the selected item and manage labels/tags (e.g., add a “Confidential” label)
Open shared items in the File Viewer for reading or editing, depending on permissions

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a shared file’s name to open it in the File Viewer.
After choosing 'Add to My Files' (if supported), navigate to the location of the new shortcut in My Files.

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Shared with me' header navigation link.

pages.name: Trash
pages.description: Trash view where deleted items are listed and can be restored or permanently removed. Supports inspection of deletion details to aid recovery decisions.
primary_functions: Show all deleted files and folders that are currently in the Trash
Sort trashed items by deleted date, name, size, or original location
Select trashed items to restore them to their original folders
Select one or more items and permanently delete them from the account
Open details for trashed items (e.g., original path, deletion date) to help decide whether to restore or purge them

outgoing_connections.trigger: After restoring an item, choose 'View in folder' to open its original parent folder in My Files.

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Trash' header navigation link.

pages.name: File Viewer
pages.description: In-browser viewer/editor for individual files such as documents, spreadsheets, and PDFs, with version history, sharing controls, labels, and offline toggle.
primary_functions: Display the content of selected files (e.g., Roadmap.docx, Team Handbook.pdf) in an appropriate viewer or editor
Provide a toolbar with actions such as share, copy link, download (if allowed), print, and make available offline
Open a version history panel showing previous versions by timestamp, and allow viewing or restoring a specific version (e.g., January 15, 2024 at 10:00 AM)
Allow basic editing for editable file types (documents, spreadsheets) where applicable, with autosave or manual save controls
Show file details (name, location, size, labels) and quick access to labels/tags for categorization
Provide navigation back to the originating list (My Files, Search Results, Recent, Shared With Me) after closing the viewer

incoming_params.description: ID of the StorageItem file to open in the viewer.
Logical source of navigation such as 'index', 'search', 'recent', 'shared', or 'storage', used to determine where to return when closing the viewer.
Optional file version ID to preselect or open within the version history panel.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click 'Close' or 'Back to My Files' after opening a file from a My Files folder.
Click 'Back to results' when the viewer was opened from Search Results.
Click 'Back to Recent' when the viewer was opened from the Recent Items page.
Click 'Back to Shared with me' when the viewer was opened from the Shared With Me page.
Click 'Back to Storage' when the viewer was opened from a Storage & Usage cleanup flow.

access_methods.description: Accessed by navigating to file_viewer.html with an 'id' query parameter from any listing page.
Can also be opened directly via externally shared links that include the file ID.

pages.name: Settings
pages.description: User and application settings page for configuring preferences related to files, sharing, offline access, notifications, and localization.
primary_functions: Adjust general preferences such as default sort order, default view (list or grid), and language or region settings
Configure default sharing behaviors, such as default link access level and whether downloads are allowed by default on shared links
Manage offline storage preferences, including maximum offline size and behavior when disk space is low
Control notification settings related to sharing, version changes, and storage limits
Access account information and links to legal documents (Privacy Policy, Terms of Service) and support resources

incoming_params.description: Optional settings subsection identifier (e.g., 'sharing', 'offline', 'notifications') to deep-link directly to a specific panel.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Help & Support' link from within the Settings page to learn more about configuration options.
Click a 'Privacy Policy' link from the Settings page.
Click a 'Terms of Service' link from the Settings page.

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Settings' footer navigation link.
Specific settings sections can be opened directly using the 'section' query parameter.

pages.name: Help & Support
pages.description: Central help center with guides and FAQs for using the cloud storage and file sharing features, plus search and links to contact support.
primary_functions: Browse categorized help topics and FAQs covering organization, sharing, version history, storage management, and offline access
Search help content to quickly find instructions for specific tasks (e.g., how to share a folder with an expiry date, how to restore a file version)
Provide step‑by‑step mini‑guides linked to relevant UI areas (My Files, Storage, File Viewer, Shared With Me)
Link to contact/support options for additional assistance if answers are not found in the help articles

incoming_params.description: Optional help topic identifier to preselect a specific article or category when opening the help center.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Contact support' or 'Still need help?' call-to-action from a help topic.

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Help' header navigation link.
Also accessible via the 'Help & Support' footer link.
Deep-linked help topics can be opened by providing a topicId query parameter.

pages.name: About
pages.description: Information about the cloud storage service, its purpose, and key features, including security and reliability notes.
primary_functions: Describe the service’s mission, focus on secure file storage, sharing, and collaboration for a single user
Highlight core capabilities such as version history, granular sharing permissions, public links with expiry, and offline access
Provide high‑level information about technology, reliability, and security practices
Link to related pages such as Privacy Policy, Terms of Service, and Contact

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Privacy Policy' link from the About page.
Click a 'Terms of Service' link from the About page.
Click a 'Contact us' or 'Get in touch' link from the About page.

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'About' footer navigation link.

pages.name: Privacy Policy
pages.description: Legal page outlining how user data and files are collected, stored, and used, including rights and contact information for privacy inquiries.
primary_functions: Explain what personal and usage data is collected and for what purpose
Describe how files and metadata are stored, protected, and retained
Clarify user rights around data access, export, and deletion
Provide contact information or procedures for privacy‑related inquiries

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Contact us about privacy' or similar link within the Privacy Policy.

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Privacy Policy' footer navigation link.

pages.name: Terms of Service
pages.description: Legal terms governing the use of the cloud storage and file sharing service, including acceptable use, liabilities, and intellectual property.
primary_functions: Outline user responsibilities and acceptable use of the service
Describe limitations of liability, warranties, and service availability commitments
Cover intellectual property, content ownership, and license terms
Refer to the Privacy Policy and provide information on how terms changes are communicated

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Privacy Policy' cross-link from the Terms of Service page.
Click a 'Contact support' or 'Questions about these terms?' link.

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Terms of Service' footer navigation link.

pages.name: Contact
pages.description: Contact and feedback page for reaching support or providing suggestions, with category selection and links back to self-service help.
primary_functions: Provide a simple contact form for support requests or feedback
Display relevant contact details such as support email addresses or links to an external support portal
Allow users to categorize their message (e.g., technical issue, billing question, feature request) to help route support
Link back to Help & Support for self‑service options before contacting support

incoming_params.description: Optional preselected contact category key (e.g., 'technical_issue', 'billing_question', 'feature_request') when navigating from other pages.

outgoing_connections.trigger: Click a 'Back to Help & Support' link after submitting a request or when choosing to self-serve instead.

access_methods.description: Accessed via the 'Contact' footer navigation link.
The 'category' query parameter can be used to preselect a contact category when opening the page.
The contact form submits via submitContactRequest and remains on contact.html to show confirmation or errors.

header_links.description: Items that others have shared with you
Storage and usage overview with large file management
Deleted items that can be restored or permanently removed

footer_links.description: Details on how your data and files are handled
Legal terms for using the service
